message:
fix(lab): proper cfg-gated suppression for clock skew variable

The inject_clock_skew method used _old_nanos (underscore prefix) to
suppress unused-variable warnings when tracing-integration was
disabled, but this also suppressed warnings when the variable was
genuinely unused.

Fix: remove the underscore prefix so the variable has its natural
name, and add an explicit #[cfg(not(feature = "tracing-integration"))]
let _ = old_nanos; suppression that only fires when tracing is off.
Removes the now-unnecessary clippy::no_effect_underscore_binding
allow attribute comment explaining the workaround.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
